title: Patriots sign offensive tackle James Hudson
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots signed offensive tackle James Hudson, who previously played 60 NFL games and started 19 times, mainly at right tackle.
Hudson was released by the Giants earlier this month and brings depth to the Patriots' offensive line.
He joined New England with a chance to compete for a roster spot as the team monitors its offensive line options.

title: As waiting game continues with James Hagens, Bruins coach has no issue adding prospects to lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins prospect James Hagens concludes his sophomore season at Boston College, awaiting a professional contract decision.
Hagens, the 2025 NHL Draft's seventh pick, recorded 47 points in 34 games this season with Boston College.
Bruins coach Marco Sturm is open to integrating younger players into the lineup, pending contract outcomes.

title: ESPN reporter explains why June 1st could be a big day for teams looking to trade for A.J. Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Trading A.J. Brown after June 1 could save the Eagles $27.147 million in dead cap space.
The New England Patriots remain interested in A.J. Brown as a potential wide receiver option.
ESPN suggests a trade involving New England's first-round pick for Brown may occur after June 1.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her mother’s fight against cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Allyson Bogdanski, a 45-year-old from Medford, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon honoring her mother's battle against breast cancer.
Her mother, treated at Dana-Farber, is a two-time breast cancer survivor whose resilience continues to inspire Bogdanski.
Running the marathon symbolizes gratitude to Dana-Farber's care team and her mother's enduring strength and courage.

title: GoFundMe for Boston officer charged in fatal shooting soars past $400K
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Officer Nicholas O'Malley charged with manslaughter in the March 11 shooting of Stephenson King in Roxbury.
A GoFundMe for Officer O'Malley has raised over $407,000 to support his family during legal proceedings.
Boston's police community and city councilors express support but call for release of bodycam footage for transparency.

title: AI attack ads are hitting Massachusetts political campaigns
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers consider bills to regulate AI in political ads amid concerns of misleading content.
Representative Marc Lombardo's AI-generated attack ad targets opponent Daniel Darris-O’Connor in Billerica election.
Gubernatorial candidate Brian Shortsleeve used AI to synthesize Governor Healey's voice in an ad, raising ethical concerns.

title: GoFundMe launched for Mass. family after teen dies in crash from overpass
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Travis Teixeira, 18, from Ashburnham, died after his car fell from a Route 2 overpass in Leominster, authorities report.
A GoFundMe campaign has raised over $70,000 to support Teixeira's family with funeral expenses and other financial needs.
The fundraiser highlights Teixeira as a talented athlete and student, emphasizing the family's need for community support during this time.

title: ‘Stay strong’: David Ortiz’s defiant declaration in the wake of tragedy still resonates with Boston
author: Chad Finn
bullets: David Ortiz's 2013 speech at Fenway Park followed the Boston Marathon bombings, resonating with the city during a difficult time.
Ortiz expressed gratitude to officials and declared, 'This is our [expletive] city,' reaffirming Boston's resilience.
The Federal Communications Commission did not penalize Ortiz for his language, acknowledging the speech's heartfelt impact.

title: Holy Cross student missing after canoe capsizes in Connecticut
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dominick Tucci, 20, of Holy Cross is missing after canoe capsized on Lake Hayward in East Haddam, Connecticut.
One of two men in the canoe was rescued and treated at a hospital; Dominick Tucci remains missing.
Search efforts involving water, air, and dive teams were suspended Sunday and expected to resume Monday.

title: The unsolved murder of Kendric Price, a beloved youth mentor gunned down on his childhood street in Dorchester
author: Emily Sweeney, The Boston Globe
bullets: Kendric Price, a youth mentor, was fatally shot on Greenwood Street in Dorchester in March 2019. His murder remains unsolved.
Price, 32, founded Big Business Network using basketball to teach kids about finances and coached at local high schools.
Boston police continue to investigate and urge anyone with information about the case to contact homicide detectives.
